f70efea7-fadc-4d40-8706-17eabae60e6e.description: This three-hour, hands-on workshop shows how to use the latest ChatGPT models as a reliable companion in accessibility testing. Participants will learn to interpret coding choices, map issues to WCAG 2.2 with clear rationale, generate practical remediation in the target tech stack, draft actionable audit narratives, research and link to techniques and failures, and streamline client communications. We finish by building a simple bookmarklet and a starter browser extension that bring AI assistance into the page under test.
f70efea7-fadc-4d40-8706-17eabae60e6e.name: AI as Your Accessibility Co-Pilot

Participants will learn to interpret coding choices, map issues to WCAG 2.2 with clear rationale, generate practical remediation in the target tech stack, draft actionable audit narratives, research and link to techniques and failures, and streamline client communications. We finish by building a simple bookmarklet and a starter browser extension that bring AI assistance into the page under test.

8db03f83-d6f4-4218-ac86-bda9e77a548c.description: Assistive Technology Basics includes a wide discussion of the variety of assistive technology, including the vast array seen here at the CSUN Assistive Technology Conference. This workshop is especially useful to newcomers to the fields of assistive technology and disabilities.
8db03f83-d6f4-4218-ac86-bda9e77a548c.name: Assistive Technology Basics

df19e55c-60b7-46da-8812-90686950d4a4.description: With the ADA Title II digital accessibility compliance deadline just weeks away, colleges, universities, and public agencies face a pivotal moment. While deadlines spur action, true success depends on what comes after. This half-day workshop offers a managed approach: understanding of the revised final rule as the foundation leading to strategies for sustaining compliance, building governance structures, and embedding accessibility practices into daily operations. Participants will learn from systemwide and campus-level case studies, then apply structured planning tools to map out next steps for their own institutions.
df19e55c-60b7-46da-8812-90686950d4a4.name: Beyond the Deadline: Manage Digital Accessibility Under Title II
df19e55c-60b7-46da-8812-90686950d4a4.richTextDescription: {"format":"draftjs-nucleus","version":1,"content":{"blocks":[{"key":"4pit6","text":"With the ADA Title II digital accessibility compliance deadline just weeks away, colleges, universities, and public agencies face a pivotal moment. Participants will learn from systemwide and campus-level case studies, then apply structured planning tools to map out next steps for their own institutions. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}}
df19e55c-60b7-46da-8812-90686950d4a4.plainTextDescription: With the ADA Title II digital accessibility compliance deadline just weeks away, colleges, universities, and public agencies face a pivotal moment. While deadlines spur action, true success depends on what comes after. This half-day workshop offers a managed approach: understanding of the revised final rule as the foundation leading to strategies for sustaining compliance, building governance structures, and embedding accessibility practices into daily operations. Participants will learn from systemwide and campus-level case studies, then apply structured planning tools to map out next steps for their own institutions.

83b15b90-c3e8-4409-a447-bab9a63e9b7c.description: Assessments offer a platform for service delivery that provides consistent, usable data, justification, and accountability for the organization, end user, and service provider. It is essential to effectively match a user’s needs, characteristics, and preferences to the correct technology. The purpose of the workshop is to provide AT practitioners with an overview of and experience with AT assessment methodology and practice. Participants will be provided an in-depth look at and in-workshop use of several assistive technology (AT) assessment tools that can be utilized at various stages of the lifespan.
83b15b90-c3e8-4409-a447-bab9a63e9b7c.name: AT Assessment Principles and Practices

The purpose of the workshop is to provide AT practitioners with an overview of and experience with AT assessment methodology and practice. Participants will be provided an in-depth look at and in-workshop use of several assistive technology (AT) assessment tools that can be utilized at various stages of the lifespan.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}}
83b15b90-c3e8-4409-a447-bab9a63e9b7c.plainTextDescription: Assessments offer a platform for service delivery that provides consistent, usable data, justification, and accountability for the organization, end user, and service provider. It is essential to effectively match a user’s needs, characteristics, and preferences to the correct technology. The purpose of the workshop is to provide AT practitioners with an overview of and experience with AT assessment methodology and practice. Participants will be provided an in-depth look at and in-workshop use of several assistive technology (AT) assessment tools that can be utilized at various stages of the lifespan.

8486142d-acbb-4b4c-9d7f-dc3a28e5313d.description: New to digital accessibility? This half-day workshop provides a practical, jargon free introduction to digital accessibility for educators and professionals who want to create more accessible digital experiences. Participants will learn techniques for building accessible experiences and practice identifying common issues through hands-on exercises. We will demystify key terminology like WCAG standards, conformance levels, VPATs and ACRs and review key foundations like document accessibility and semantic HTML.  We’ll also explore how accessibility impacts procurement decisions and why evaluating vendor accessibility claims is critical. By the end of the session, attendees will have a clear understanding of accessibility fundamentals and actionable strategies to build more inclusive digital experiences.
8486142d-acbb-4b4c-9d7f-dc3a28e5313d.name: Demystifying Digital Accessibility: Starting with the Basics

We will demystify key terminology like WCAG standards, conformance levels, VPATs and ACRs and review key foundations like document accessibility and semantic HTML.  We’ll also explore how accessibility impacts procurement decisions and why evaluating vendor accessibility claims is critical. By the end of the session, attendees will have a clear understanding of accessibility fundamentals and actionable strategies to build more inclusive digital experiences.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}}
8486142d-acbb-4b4c-9d7f-dc3a28e5313d.plainTextDescription: New to digital accessibility? This half-day workshop provides a practical, jargon free introduction to digital accessibility for educators and professionals who want to create more accessible digital experiences. Participants will learn techniques for building accessible experiences and practice identifying common issues through hands-on exercises. We will demystify key terminology like WCAG standards, conformance levels, VPATs and ACRs and review key foundations like document accessibility and semantic HTML.  We’ll also explore how accessibility impacts procurement decisions and why evaluating vendor accessibility claims is critical. By the end of the session, attendees will have a clear understanding of accessibility fundamentals and actionable strategies to build more inclusive digital experiences.

d8a7bb79-c750-419e-8d6a-fc210987702c.description: This workshop is designed to empower developers to use AI-integrated IDEs, such as Cursor, to generate accessible code. As artificial intelligence increasingly assists in coding tasks, there is both an opportunity & a responsibility to ensure that the outputs of these tools align with accessibility & inclusive design principles.
d8a7bb79-c750-419e-8d6a-fc210987702c.name: Setting Up AI-Integrated IDEs for Accessible Code by Default
d8a7bb79-c750-419e-8d6a-fc210987702c.richTextDescription: {"format":"draftjs-nucleus","version":1,"content":{"blocks":[{"key":"fh48r","text":"This workshop is designed to empower developers to use AI-integrated IDEs, such as Cursor, to generate accessible code. As artificial intelligence increasingly assists in coding tasks, there is both an opportunity & a responsibility to ensure that the outputs of these tools align with accessibility & inclusive design principles.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}}
d8a7bb79-c750-419e-8d6a-fc210987702c.plainTextDescription: This workshop is designed to empower developers to use AI-integrated IDEs, such as Cursor, to generate accessible code. As artificial intelligence increasingly assists in coding tasks, there is both an opportunity & a responsibility to ensure that the outputs of these tools align with accessibility & inclusive design principles.

eecc3495-aa46-436a-8eb2-9646dfe6f3ba.description: This workshop provides an in-depth overview of the European Accessibility Act (EAA), a landmark directive requiring all 27 EU member states to implement minimum accessibility standards for products and services across public and private sectors. Participants will explore the EAA’s background, principles, key requirements, implementation timeline, and relationship to other global accessibility frameworks such as Section 508, the ADA, and WCAG. The session also covers the new EN 301 549 standard, monitoring processes, and challenges in achieving harmonized implementation across Europe, concluding with an open discussion on current developments and practical implications.
eecc3495-aa46-436a-8eb2-9646dfe6f3ba.name: The European Accessibility Act (EAA) – Essential Information

Participants will explore the EAA’s background, principles, key requirements, implementation timeline, and relationship to other global accessibility frameworks such as Section 508, the ADA, and WCAG. The session also covers the new EN 301 549 standard, monitoring processes, and challenges in achieving harmonized implementation across Europe, concluding with an open discussion on current developments and practical implications.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}}
eecc3495-aa46-436a-8eb2-9646dfe6f3ba.plainTextDescription: This workshop provides an in-depth overview of the European Accessibility Act (EAA), a landmark directive requiring all 27 EU member states to implement minimum accessibility standards for products and services across public and private sectors. Participants will explore the EAA’s background, principles, key requirements, implementation timeline, and relationship to other global accessibility frameworks such as Section 508, the ADA, and WCAG. The session also covers the new EN 301 549 standard, monitoring processes, and challenges in achieving harmonized implementation across Europe, concluding with an open discussion on current developments and practical implications.
